title: Aloo Tikki

ingredients.title: For the Tikki
items: Potatoes (flour variety) – 500 g, boiled, peeled, and cooled completely
Fresh coriander leaves – 2 tbsp, finely chopped
Dry mango powder (amchur) – ½ tsp (or 1 tsp lemon juice)

ingredients.title: Optional Stuffing (for restaurant-style surprise filling)
items: Green peas – 50 g, boiled and lightly mashed

instruction.title: Prep the Potatoes
steps: After boiling, cool completely to avoid excess moisture.
Mash finely with a potato masher—ensure no lumps for a smooth texture.

instruction.title: Make the Stuffing (optional but chef’s touch)
steps: Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan. Add peas, coriander powder, garam masala, and salt.
Cook for 1–2 min, then let cool.

instruction.title: Prepare the Tikki Mixture
steps: In a large mixing bowl, combine mashed potatoes, green chili, coriander leaves, ginger, red chili powder, roasted cumin, amchur, chaat masala, cornflour, and salt.
Mix gently; avoid over-mixing to keep the texture fluffy.

instruction.title: Shape
steps: If using stuffing: flatten one portion, place 1 tsp stuffing in the center, and close edges to form a patty.

instruction.title: Cooking
steps: Heat a heavy-bottom skillet or tawa on medium heat.
Add oil/ghee (a mix gives better aroma and golden crust).
Place tikkis and shallow fry 3–4 minutes per side until golden and crisp.
Do not overcrowd the pan; cook in batches.

chefTips: Binding: Adjust cornflour depending on moisture level of potatoes. If too wet, add breadcrumbs.
Crispness secret: Fry on medium heat first to cook through, then increase heat for the last 30 seconds for a crunchy crust.
Holding shape: Refrigerate shaped tikkis for 20 minutes before frying.
Serving suggestion: Plate with tamarind chutney, mint chutney, a dollop of yogurt, sev, and pomegranate pearls for a chaat-style presentation.
